description_long: This is a small, traditional wooden canoe with a weathered, dark brown finish, featuring a pointed bow and a slightly squared stern. The canoe has three visible crosswise seats or thwarts, providing structural support and seating for passengers. Along the top edges, gunwales run the length of the vessel, reinforcing the sides and adding rigidity. Two metal carrying handles are attached to the gunwales near the bow and stern, allowing for easy transport by hand. The interior is open and spacious enough to accommodate two to three people, with visible wood grain and signs of use. The exterior is unadorned, with no visible registration numbers or markings.
description: A weathered wooden canoe with three seats, gunwales, and metal carrying handles, finished in dark brown. The open interior and sturdy build suggest it is suitable for small groups.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the canoe, three seats, and both carrying handles on the gunwales.
description_view_1: Front (bow) view highlighting the pointed shape, interior seating arrangement, and the symmetry of the gunwales.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with clear visibility of the seats and handles.
description_view_3: Rear (stern) view showing the squared-off end, interior seating, and the alignment of the thwarts.
